content: Just as the human brain uses active, reactivated memory traces to consolidate learned knowledge across distributed neural populations, a scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning can be redesigned to mimic this mechanism: instead of static shared storage, agents dynamically reactivate and revise shared memory traces based on their own active learning uncertainty, aligning individual exploration with collective knowledge gain. This addresses the common problem of redundant exploration in large multi-agent active learning systems by leveraging the human brain’s evolved efficiency for coordinated active learning through shared memory.
source_topics: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
trigger_topic: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
